title: pastinaca kochii subsp. latifolia
intro: Pastinaca kochii subsp. latifolia, also known as the broad-leaved parsnip, is a subspecies of the Pastinaca kochii plant, which is a perennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of Pastinaca kochii subsp. latifolia include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, are typically made from its roots and leaves, and are used to support overall health and well-being.
